model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The fixed-step cosine schedule regressed sharply on this workload, so the highest-confidence next move is to disable scheduling and return to constant learning rate behavior before testing further changes.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, the compact CNN is likely undertrained at 5e-4; increasing Adam learning rate to 1e-3 is a simple high-impact within-budget convergence test.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous LR increase improved validation loss, so a moderate further increase to 1.5e-3 tests whether this short-budget regime benefits from more aggressive optimization without a large instability jump.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss keeps improving as learning rate increases, so pushing to 2e-3 is a direct continuation of the successful within-budget optimization trend.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"2e-3 overshot, so revert to the best-known 1.5e-3 and test lighter regularization, since heavy weight decay can underfit in a 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the better-performing weight decay and test a lower Adam beta2 to increase responsiveness in the short 256-step regime.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()","new":"            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            optimizer.step()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"At the aggressive learning rate, adding light gradient clipping can reduce occasional unstable updates in this short-budget run and may recover better validation loss without changing model capacity.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"delete_exact","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)\n","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase early feature capacity with a modest channel-width bump while removing the clipping change that regressed, aiming for better representational power within the same training budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Now that width increased, test restoring the more conservative Adam second-moment decay to reduce update noise and potentially improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Revert to the better beta2 setting and test a slightly larger classifier head to better use the widened convolutional features.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger head regressed, so revert it and probe a slightly lower learning rate with the stronger BASE_CHANNELS=16 model to improve stability and validation loss.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation improved when lowering LR from 1.5e-3 to 1.3e-3, so a small further decrease to 1.2e-3 is the next targeted sweep around the current optimum.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss continued improving as LR decreased, so test 1.1e-3 to continue the local search around the new best region.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 8e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Revert to the best learning rate and test a small reduction in weight decay to slightly relax regularization in this short-step setting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 8e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Return to the stronger regularization setting and probe an intermediate learning rate between 1.2e-3 and 1.3e-3 to refine the local optimum.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The recent setting regressed sharply, so revert to the best learning rate and test AdamW to keep adaptive updates while decoupling weight decay, which can improve short-budget optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Return to Adam (which has the best observed region here) and test slightly stronger regularization than 1e-4 to see if it improves validation under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Current result is very close to best-so-far, so perform a fine-grained regularization sweep by slightly reducing weight decay.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The slight increase in weight decay regressed, so reverting to 1e-4 returns to the strongest observed regularization setting for this configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
